definitions.definition: A system in which only one party holds power and controls the government, often through authoritarian means. This party may dominate all levels of government and restrict the activities of other parties.
A form of government in which the ruler or ruling party has complete control over all aspects of society, often using force or coercion to suppress opposition and dissent.
The process by which a single party or individual gains and maintains control over the government and society, often through the suppression of opposition and the manipulation of institutions.
The phenomenon in which one party consistently wins elections and holds power, often due to a combination of factors such as propaganda, repression, and manipulation of the electoral process.
A form of communication that is used to influence public opinion and promote a particular ideology or agenda, often through the use of biased or misleading information.
A set of beliefs or principles that guide the actions and decisions of a government or political party, often reflecting a particular worldview or vision for society.
The perception or acceptance of a government or institution as having the right to rule or govern, often based on its ability to provide stability, security, and services to the population.
The political party that holds power and controls the government, often through the possession of a majority of seats in the legislature.
Political parties that do not hold power and often critique or challenge the policies and actions of the ruling party.
Changes to the electoral system, such as the introduction of proportional representation or the reform of electoral districts, designed to promote greater representation and competition.
The suppression or restriction of information, ideas, or opinions, often through the use of laws or regulations that restrict freedom of speech and expression.
A network of institutions and mechanisms used by the ruling party to disseminate propaganda and promote its ideology, often through the use of media, education, and other channels.
A type of one-party system characterized by the dominance of a single party, often using authoritarian means, and the suppression of opposition and dissent.
A system in which the trappings of democracy, such as elections and a legislature, are present, but the ruling party exercises control and suppresses opposition, often through authoritarian means.
Competitive processes in which citizens choose their representatives or vote on policies, often through the use of secret ballots and other democratic mechanisms.
The group of individuals who hold power and control the government, often through the possession of wealth, status, and influence.
A process by which a government or ruling party mobilizes and engages citizens, often through the use of propaganda, rallies, and other mechanisms, to promote its ideology and interests.
